DistilBART_Summary: The Kindle Paperwhite is reliable, holds a charge for weeks, is lightweight and easy to hold . The e-ink screen almost feels like a page of a paperback when you swipe, and in my personal preference, I like that . E-ink display allows you to also read outside in direct sunlight, perfect for the upcoming summer months . Navigation is a bit cumbersome since there are no physical buttons, the "BACK" button in particular is missed .

Amazon Echo is a great device to use for listening to audible books or music, recording a shopping list and controlling other smart devices in the house . Alexa can play music, answer questions, play favorite radio stations, set timers in kitchen, tell jokes . Sound is good (not Bose, but acceptable) Easy to add features (calendar, To Do List, reminders, etc. can be a blast)

4K compatible and microSD card slot makes this a great device for streaming video . Would high recommend this over Fire Stick, faster and can add extra memory... Cut cable and use this as our primary tv source along with antenna for local channels . The hardest part so far is getting used to not having a DVR and not being able to fast fwd through commercials . The lack of Google Play Movies and Google Play Music is regrettable .

AmazonBasics batteries hold up really well in my son's toys, better than most so far and last a few days in my hubby's Xbox controller . They come in packs of 4, so not loose batteries rolling around . Good price but quality is not really reliable . They are the worst batteries I've ever used . I haven't tried them in any high-power devices like digital cameras or remote control cars not sure if they would be the best choice .
